title: `"/" is not a shared mount` warning when running PINP example
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying to figure out how to run rootless-podman inside rootless-podman.  After having very little success with my own container, I tried following the example [here](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container).  While that example works, it prints out a warning for me, which I think is related to the problem that I see with my own container.  I ran this by @rhatdan, and he asked me to open this issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Execute the following command:
```
podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable podman run alpine echo hello
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable podman run alpine echo hello
time="2022-08-11T22:21:01Z" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:213ec9aee27d8be045c6a92b7eac22c9a64b44558193775a1a7f626352392b49
Copying blob sha256:213ec9aee27d8be045c6a92b7eac22c9a64b44558193775a1a7f626352392b49
Copying config sha256:9c6f0724472873bb50a2ae67a9e7adcb57673a183cea8b06eb778dca859181b5
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
hello
```
(Note the warning message in the first line of the output.)

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the command to run without warnings.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
(See environment details below.)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Thu Jul 28 14:53:18 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: @rhatdan You're our podman-in-container expert, mind taking a look?

So, before this issue closes, I have a question:  should the following command work "as is"?
```
$ podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable \
    podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable \
    echo hello
```
For me, it complains:
```
Error: crun: mount `/proc` to `/proc`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```

However, if I remove the `--device /dev/fuse` from the inner container invocation, then it works.

And, this leads to the question of...should this command work?
```
$ podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman --device /dev/fuse quay.io/podman/stable \
    podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman quay.io/podman/stable \
    podman run --security-opt label=disable --user podman quay.io/podman/stable \
    echo hello
```
For me, it fails in what I take to be the innermost container invocation:
```
time="2022-08-15T14:56:01Z" level=error msg="running `/usr/bin/newuidmap 12 0 1000 1 1 1 999 1000 1001 64535`: newuidmap: Could not open proc directory for target 12\n"
Error: cannot setup namespace using "/usr/bin/newuidmap": exit status 1
```
I guess what I'm asking is, should I be able to run basically any container inside `quay.io/podman/stable`, and, if so, is there any reason why I shouldn't be able to run a container inside that one?  (I.e., is there any kind of limit or restriction the ability to nest containers?)

Thanks.

Your running a container within a user namespace, so that means the second container is bound to the UIDs of the first container.  

@giuseppe Do you remember how to get around the /proc error?
